zh.lifeSummary: 當Albert Ier de Chiny於1098年出生在比利時盧布格尼時，他的父親Otton II de Chiny33歲，母親Adélaïs de Namur32歲。他於1140年與Agnès de Bar在法國德 艾斯特姆斯布拉班特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1135年在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇利艾被列為evêque de liège。他去世於1162年9月29日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇利艾享年64歲，埋葬在法國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Albert Ier de Chiny于1098年出生在比利时盧布格尼时，他的父亲Otton II de Chiny33岁，母亲Adélaïs de Namur32岁。他于1140年与Agnès de Bar在法国德 艾斯特姆斯布拉班特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1135年在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇利艾被列为evêque de liège。他去世于1162年9月29日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇利艾享年64岁，埋葬在法国。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chiny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surnames 荊, 景, 井, 敬, 靖 and 經, see   Jing   1-6.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 程, see   Cheng   1.

variants.name: Chinn
meanings: English: from Old English cin ‘chin’, as a nickname for someone with a prominent chin or else for a clean-shaven man.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 陳 (see   Chen   1) and 錢 (see   Qian   1).

nameMeanings.name: Albert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Albert Ier de Chiny was born in 1098, in Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgium, his father, Otton II de Chiny, was 33 and his mother, Adélaïs de Namur, was 32. He married Agnès de Bar in 1140, in Brabant, Meuse, Grand Est, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. In 1135, at the age of 37, his occupation is listed as evêque de liège in Liège, Holy Roman Empire. He died on 29 September 1162, in Liège, Holy Roman Empire, at the age of 64, and was buried in France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Albert Ier de Chiny est né en 1098, à Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgique, son père, Otton II de Chiny, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Adélaïs de Namur, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Agnès de Bar en 1140, à Brabant, Meuse, Grand Est, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. En 1135, à l’âge de 37 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est evêque de liège à Liège, Saint-Empire romain. Il est décédé le 29 septembre 1162, à Liège, Saint-Empire romain, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Albert Ier de Chiny 1098 in Chiny, Luxemburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Otton II de Chiny 33 und seine Mutter Adélaïs de Namur 32. Er hat 1140 in Brabant, Meuse, Großer Osten, Frankreich Agnès de Bar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1135, als er 37 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als evêque de liège in Lüttich, Heiliges Römisches Reich aufgeführt. Er starb am 29. September 1162 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Lüttich, Heiliges Römisches Reich und wurde in Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Albert Ier de Chiny è nato il nel 1098 nel Chiny, Lussemburgo, Belgio, il suo padre, Otton II de Chiny, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Adélaïs de Namur, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Agnès de Bar nel 1140, nel Brabant, Meuse, Grand Est, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1135, all'età di 37 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come evêque de liège nel Liegi, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Morì il 29 settembre 1162, nel Liegi, Heiliges Römisches Reich, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Albert Ier de Chinyが1098年にベルギールクセンボウルグチニーで生まれた時，父Otton II de Chinyは33歳，母Adélaïs de Namurは32歳であった。1140年にフランスグランド エストメウゼブラバントでAgnès de Barと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1135年，37歳の時，ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチリeッジでの職業はevêque de liègeと記録されている。彼は1162年9月29日にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチリeッジで64歳で死去し，フランスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Albert Ier de Chiny(이)가 1098년 벨기에 루세보우르그 친이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Otton II de Chiny(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Adélaïs de Namur(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1140년 프랑스 그랜드 에스트 우스 브라반트에서 Agnès de Bar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1135년, 37세의 나이로 그의 직업은 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 리e지에서 evêque de liège이었다. 그는 1162년 9월 29일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 리e지에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Albert Ier de Chiny nasceu em 1098, em Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Otton II de Chiny, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Adélaïs de Namur, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnès de Bar em 1140, em Brabant, Meuse, Grand Est, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1135, com 37 anos, sua profissão aparece como evêque de liège em Liège, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Ele faleceu em 29 de setembro de 1162, em Liège, Heiliges Römisches Reich, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Albert Ier de Chiny родился в 1098 г. в местности чини, луксембург, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Otton II de Chiny было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Adélaïs de Namur, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnès de Bar в 1140 г. в местности брабант, меус, гранд эст, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. В 1135 году, в возрасте 37 лет,  он работал по профессии evêque de liège в местности лиeдж, хилигесь рoмищесь раич. Он умер 29 сентября 1162 г. в местности лиeдж, хилигесь рoмищесь раич в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Albert Ier de Chiny nació en 1098, en Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Otton II de Chiny, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Adélaïs de Namur, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Agnès de Bar en 1140, en Brabant, Meuse, Grand Est, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1135, a la edad de 37 años, su ocupación registrada era evêque de liège en Lieja, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Murió el 29 de septiembre de 1162, en Lieja, Heiliges Römisches Reich, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Francia.
